easy.title: Build Your First HTML Page
easy.description: Create a simple HTML page that introduces yourself. Include a heading with your name, a paragraph about your interests, and a list of your three favorite hobbies.
easy.starterCode:
<!DOCTYPE html>
<html lang="en">
<head>
    <meta charset="UTF-8">
    <meta name="viewport" content="width=device-width, initial-scale=1.0">
    <title>About Me</title>
</head>
<body>
    <!-- Add your content here -->
</body>
</html>
easy.solution:
John Doe

I am a web development enthusiast who loves learning HTML. In my free time, I enjoy coding, reading, and exploring new technologies.

My Hobbies

  • Programming
  • Reading books
  • Playing chess

medium.title: Create a Semantic HTML Page Layout
medium.description: Build a blog post layout using semantic HTML5 elements. Include a header with navigation, a main section with an article (with headings and paragraphs), an aside with related links, and a footer with copyright information.
medium.starterCode:
<!DOCTYPE html>
<html lang="en">
<head>
    <meta charset="UTF-8">
    <meta name="viewport" content="width=device-width, initial-scale=1.0">
    <title>My Blog</title>
</head>
<body>
    <!-- Build the semantic layout here -->
</body>
</html>
medium.solution:
My Tech Blog

Home Articles About Contact

Getting Started with HTML

HTML is the foundation of web development. In this post, we will explore the basics of building web pages.

Why Learn HTML?

HTML is essential for anyone who wants to build websites or work in web development. It is easy to learn and forms the basis for more advanced technologies.

Key Takeaways

Understanding HTML gives you the ability to create structured, accessible, and SEO-friendly web pages.

Related Articles

  • CSS for Beginners
  • JavaScript Fundamentals
  • Responsive Design Tips

hard.title: Build a Multi-Page Website Skeleton
hard.description: Create a 3-page website about a topic of your choice. Each page must have: consistent header and footer across all pages, a navigation bar linking to all pages, semantic structure, at least one image per page, an ordered list on one page, an unordered list on another page, a table of data on one page, and a contact form on the third page. Use only HTML — no CSS or JavaScript.
hard.starterCode:
<!-- index.html -->
<!DOCTYPE html>
<html lang="en">
<head>
    <meta charset="UTF-8">
    <meta name="viewport" content="width=device-width, initial-scale=1.0">
    <title>My Website - Home</title>
</head>
<body>
    <!-- Home page content -->
</body>
</html>
